followUps.title: Migrate downstream consumers of acronyms-catalog from file/name refs to D9 stream URL
followUps.scope: 5 downstream consumers identified in T1 substrate Step 7: schema-registry/schemas/patent/glossary.schema.json — update $ref to https://schemas.luh.tech/acronyms-catalog.json; LuhTech-business/filings/glossary.md + glossary.yml — link to stream URL; LuhTech-business/docs/LUHTECH-PATENT-FOUNDATION-CORRECTIONS-2026-05-06.md — update reference; LuhTech-business/.roadmap/follow-up-register.json — close stale catalog-absence FU entries.
followUps.coordination: Ectropy-Business follow-up-register should also note this as a cross-repo concern once D9 stream URL is validated live.

followUps.title: Resolve cosmetic jsonschema deprecation warning for decision-log DEC validation
followUps.scope: Step 17 of T2 Phase 1: DEC log validation passes but triggers jsonschema network auto-fetch deprecation warning. Root cause: decision-log.schema.v2 $refs luhtech-enums; the Draft7Validator may not propagate the local registry through to internal sub-validator $ref resolution. Audit referencing.Registry propagation in validate.py; confirm luhtech-enums $id coverage in schema-map; consider explicit pre-resolution walk.
followUps.coordination: Likely folds into T3 substrate prep — fixing propagation lets T3 instance validations run clean. Cosmetic only; validation passes.

resolution.summary: Resolved without code change. Root cause was substrate-ordering during T2 Phase 1: schema-map.json had not yet been generated when DEC log validation ran in Step 17, so the local registry was empty and luhtech-enums $refs fell through to jsonschema network auto-fetch. T2 landed the schema-map; T3 substrate Step 7 reproduced validation under post-T2 registry and observed zero warnings. Lesson: regenerate indices BEFORE running dependent validations.

followUps.title: Bundle XaaS→SNx migration into T5 — directory renames, URN segments, description strings
followUps.scope: T4 substrate confirmed XaaS terminology survives in three Ectropy-domain surfaces: (a) directory names caas/, gaas/, paas/, maas/ holding 6 schemas; (b) URN $id segments encoded into those path components; (c) description-string prose in usf/usf-work-packet, maas/material-supplier, schema-index.json. Bundle into T5 (urn-to-url) so directory rename + URN segment update + description polish land in one coherent mutation pass. Pre-T5: resolve SNx naming-form decision (csx/ vs corsx/ vs full noun); scan Ectropy product code for hard-coded caas/gaas/paas/maas imports that break on rename.
followUps.coordination: SNx naming-form decision needed from Erik before T5 substrate. Also coordinate with Ectropy-Business and BPC on import paths.

resolution.summary: Completed across T5.1 + T5.3 + T5.4. T5.1 (Ectropy-ai/schemas#5) renamed 4 XaaS directories to SNx (caas→corsx, gaas→govsx, paas→paysx, maas→matsx), converted 57 URN $ids to URL form, updated schema-index.json slugs, corrected description prose. T5.3 (Ectropy-Business#143) migrated consumer-side URN→URL. T5.2 struck. T5.4 aligned schema-registry schema-map. Final: 99/99 D2 compliance.

followUps.title: Define deprecation policy + execute migrate v1-to-v3-deprecate for v-suffix families
followUps.scope: T4 substrate Step 4 surfaced 5 multi-variant schema families: luhtech-enums (2 variants), decision-log (2), roadmap (10), roadmap-business (4 incl v3.0.draft), venture-summary (2). Step 5 showed v-suffix variants are not always old: luhtech-enums v2 IS the active one (6 live $refs from graph/brand/workflow-registry). Three policy decisions before execute: (1) deprecation marker form — boolean field, status enum, or _archived/ subdir; (2) per-family current-variant confirmation via $ref evidence; (3) $ref migration plan for consumers of variants to be deprecated.

followUps.title: Remove unauthorized DaaS parenthetical from ectropy-ai/schemas/schema-index.json
followUps.scope: schema-index.json carries 'Decision Lifecycle (DaaS)' as a category name. Erik confirmed: DaaS was never approved — it is not in the canonical catalog section J. The Decision Lifecycle category itself is real (decision/ subdir, 5 schemas); only the (DaaS) parenthetical is wrong. Recommended fix: drop the parenthetical. Bundle into FU-ECTROPY-XAAS-MIGRATION-1 Ectropy editing pass at T5. Also audit VaaS, CaaS, PaaS, MaaS, RaaS/HaaS category name parentheticals for the same issue.
followUps.coordination: Bundled into FU-ECTROPY-XAAS-MIGRATION-1 — do the schema-index.json cleanup in the same T5 editing pass as XaaS directory renames. No new DSx acronym needed — Decision Lifecycle as a category does not need an SNx-style parenthetical.

resolution.summary: Resolved during T5.1 schema-index slug migration. DaaS slug renamed to 'decision' (directory-aligned; decision/ schemas are data primitives, not service exchanges). VaaS slug renamed to 'voxel' by the same logic. DaaS parenthetical stripped from the 'Decision Lifecycle' category display name. No DSx acronym added to catalog because 'Decision Lifecycle' names a schema primitive tier, not a service-exchange interface.

followUps.title: Review ventureType enum — propose 5-bucket consolidation (Platform/Hardware/Service/Marketplace/Holding)
followUps.scope: Per D-T5-C (deferred from T5 arc): luhtech-enums.schema.v2.json ventureType enum includes SaaS, API, Platform, Hardware, Marketplace, Holding. Proposed 5-bucket consolidation: Platform | Hardware | Service | Marketplace | Holding — absorbs SaaS + API into Service (broader model covering SaaS, managed-services, professional-services). Requires: audit current usage across 8 venture roadmap.json files; draft migration mapping (SaaS→Service, API→Platform); file DEC with breaking-change semantics; schema change additive then deprecation pass.

followUps.title: Wire schemas.luh.tech/ectropy/* hosting to actually serve Ectropy schemas
followUps.scope: T5 arc closed L11 SH-1 by migrating 57 Ectropy schemas from URN to URL $id form at https://schemas.luh.tech/ectropy/<path>. T5.4 verified the schemas are correct and the schema-map indexes them. However: curl https://schemas.luh.tech/ectropy/corsx/takt-wagon.schema.json returns 404. The luh-tech/schema-registry Pages deployment serves only this repo's schemas/ directory; the actual Ectropy schema files live in Ectropy-ai/schemas (separate repo). Every Ectropy $id URL is therefore unresolved at the HTTP layer. Consequence: D2 rationale (URL form resolves automatically in ajv and standard JSON Schema tools) is paper-only for L11 until hosting is wired. Decision options: (A) schema-registry Pages workflow ingests Ectropy-ai/schemas as a build step and serves under /ectropy/<path>; (B) Cloudflare routes schemas.luh.tech/ectropy/* to a separate Pages site backed by Ectropy-ai/schemas; (C) Ectropy gets its own subdomain — requires re-migrating all 57 $ids, breaks D-T5-A; (D) git submodule the Ectropy schemas into schema-registry. Substrate before DEC: inventory current consumers that fetch Ectropy schemas via HTTP (if zero, downgrade to P3). Tenth discipline-catch from T5 arc: URL-form migrations must include HTTPS resolve verification as a gate criterion, not just $id field shape and registry indexing. [2026-05-20 audit gate CLOSED] Phase-B1 consumer audit (source: phase-b1-ectropy-http-fetch-consumer-audit) scanned 11 live repos across 6 file-type categories. Zero HTTP consumers found. The two TypeScript matches in apps/mcp-server/src/services/ are non-consuming $schema annotations on in-memory data records — JSON Schema canonical-identifier pattern with no fetch/axios/HTTP client invocation. FU gate criterion met: priority downgraded P2→P3. [2026-05-21 upgrade P3→P1] 24 active EB .roadmap/features/ instance files reference ectropy/feature.schema.json and ectropy/interfaces.schema.json via local vendored copies whose $id is https://schemas.luh.tech/ectropy/*. These URLs return 404 — hosting not wired. FU-EB-RELATIVE-SCHEMA-PATH-CLEANUP-1 (P1 in EB) cannot close until ectropy/* paths serve 200. The 'zero HTTP consumers' claim that justified P3 downgrade (2026-05-20 audit gate) is no longer accurate — 24 consumers exist; they consumed via local vendored copies, not direct HTTP. P3→P1 upgrade effective 2026-05-21.

resolution.summary: Option A implemented via SR#35 (d3d1486): deploy-pages.yml now ingests ectropy-ai/schemas and publishes under _site/ectropy/. Phase C probe confirmed: 49/52 index-listed schemas serve HTTP 200 at schemas.luh.tech/ectropy/*; schema-index.json serves 200; portfolio controls unchanged. 3 documented non-200s are phantom index entries (phone-agent-config, rag-context, siltana/robot) — files not present in ectropy-ai/schemas, tagged commercial/separate-repo in schema-index. Phase D confirmed ectropy/feature.schema.json and ectropy/interfaces.schema.json serve 200 (validated: ros-mro/interfaces.json retarget passed Draft 7). Hosting is live. Remaining EB retarget blockers (22 FEATURE files) are schema const constraint issues tracked under FU-SR-ECTROPY-FEATURE-SCHEMA-CONST-CONSTRAINT-1, not hosting gaps.

followUps.title: Fix oneOf integer+number defect in roadmap-business.schema.v2.1.json
followUps.scope: roadmap-business.schema.v2.1.json at schemas.luh.tech has a Draft 7 validation defect: the financials.preSeed.variables[].value field uses oneOf: [{type: integer}, {type: number}]. In JSON Schema Draft 7, every integer is also a number, so any integer value (e.g. 750000) is valid under both branches simultaneously, causing oneOf to fail. Surfaced during relative-schema-cleanup.py Phase 4 validation: '.roadmap/roadmap-business.json failed: 750000 is valid under each of {type: integer}, {type: number}'. Fix: replace oneOf with plain {type: number} (or remove the integer variant entirely — number already covers all integers). No instance data changes needed. The fix should land as roadmap-business.schema.v2.1.1.json (patch semver bump) or inline edit of v2.1.json if the immutability policy allows patch corrections to published schemas — per forward-versioning standard the latter is preferred for bug fixes that do not change schema semantics. Once fixed and re-published at schemas.luh.tech, .roadmap/roadmap-business.json in EB can be retargeted from URN form and will validate cleanly.

followUps.title: Remove const $schema constraint in ectropy/feature.schema.json blocking URL-form retargets
followUps.scope: ectropy/feature.schema.json at schemas.luh.tech has a const constraint on its $schema property: {"type": "string", "const": "../../schemas/feature.schema.json"}. This forces every FEATURE.json instance to declare $schema as the local relative path '../../schemas/feature.schema.json'. When Phase D attempted to retarget 21 FEATURE.json files to the canonical URL form (schemas.luh.tech/ectropy/feature.schema.json), Draft 7 validation failed with '$schema: ...feature.schema.json was expected at [$schema]' — the const constraint rejected the URL form. Fix: remove the $schema property constraint from ectropy/feature.schema.json (or broaden it to accept the URL form). This is a schema-authoring defect that prevents D2 compliance for all EB feature instances. 21 EB FEATURE.json files blocked. Detected 2026-05-21 Phase D via relative-schema-cleanup.py Catch #22.

followUps.title: Promote RULE-PIPELINE-PROJECTION-CONTRACT-1 working-model -> automated via F5 fusion dispatch-verbatim-transport
followUps.scope: RULE-PIPELINE-PROJECTION-CONTRACT-1 sits at lifecycleStatus working-model + tierState.activeTier working-model with terminalTier automated. Rule statement requires dispatch-verbatim-transport from chat agent to terminal agent. Automation path documented in instance notes field as PENDING F5 fusion: needs scoping to determine where projection contract's automated tier lives (likely dispatch tooling or agent harness layer, not schema-registry workflow surface). Closes F5 dispatch-verbatim-transport into this FU when scope authoring is undertaken.

followUps.title: CI workflow validating all rule and deliverable instances against their schemas on every PR
followUps.scope: Promote the validate-on-merge pattern from 'we ran ajv-cli in the dispatch' to 'CI enforces it on every PR.' Landed as .github/workflows/instance-validation.yml. Workflow iterates over schemas/_instances/rules/*.json and schemas/_instances/deliverables/*.json, validates each against its schema (rule.schema.json or deliverable.schema.json) via ajv-cli with forbidden-audit-paint-names allOf ref loaded. Fires on PR + push to main on paths schemas/_instances/** + the two schemas.

resolution.closedDuring: Foundation arc FU-D-1 (2026-06-01) — instance-validation.yml landed on main
resolution.summary: Workflow .github/workflows/instance-validation.yml landed via FU-D-1 deliverable during foundation arc. Validates schemas/_instances/rules/*.json against rule.schema.json and schemas/_instances/deliverables/*.json against deliverable.schema.json with forbidden-audit-paint-names allOf $ref loaded via ajv-cli@5 -r flag. Fires on PR and push to main on paths schemas/_instances/** plus the two schemas. Registered for substrate completeness per L9 reconcile hybrid coverage path; closed-on-arrival, no follow-up action required.

followUps.title: Future bump of decision-log.schema to add rule-promotion semantic fields
followUps.scope: Current decision-log.schema.v2.json accommodates rule-promotion DECs via tags + impactedDeliverables + evidence fields (verified in this deliverable). Future bump candidates for stronger semantic typing: (a) impactedRules array field paralleling impactedDeliverables; (b) decisionType enum discriminator distinguishing rule-promotion / rule-affirmation / arc-initiation from general decisions; (c) ruleTransitionEvidence object linking DEC to specific rule transition entry. Bump is non-breaking (additive optional fields). Not blocking — current schema accommodates the work; bump improves queryability and structured semantics. Open for future arc scoping when decision-log schema evolution is planned.

resolution.summary: Additive enum extension. Driven by EB downstream consumer d-2026-06-03-eb-catalog-objectstorage-hygiene which required terraform-state purpose to capture TF state backend buckets in env-prod.objectStorage. Filed-and-closed in same PR per cross-chat governance pattern. Closed by erik-luh. followUps.title: Add credentialItem.livenessProbe field to resource-access.schema.json
followUps.openedDuring: EB cross-chat handoff canvas F0B7U37MSDC (2026-06-02 18:29 PDT) — GAP-EXTEND P1 surfaced from d-2026-06-02-prod-spaces-credentials-graph audit
followUps.scope: Additive backwards-compatible extension to credentialItem definition in resource-access.schema.json. Adds livenessProbe block: lastVerifiedReachable (ISO-8601 timestamp), lastVerifiedResult (enum: success, auth-failed, network-failed, not-tested), verifiedBy (deliverable-id or operator). Substrate evidence: two BW credentials in luhtechnology.com vault (d3ef349b DO_SPACES_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY_PROD, 8adf7118 DO_BUCKET_ECTROPY_MIGRATE) are structurally complete but fail DO auth with InvalidAccessKeyId — current schema has no field to distinguish dead credentials from live ones in the access graph. EB's STEP 1-PRE-D bucket-access matrix data has no schema home and lives only in chat carry-forward. Proposed shape illustrative; SR refines as canonical authority on credentialItem evolution.

resolution.summary: Schema extension landed: livenessProbe optional nested object added under credentialItem definition in schemas/resource-access.schema.json at version 1.1.0 (minor-bump-for-additive per PR #102 + PR #106 precedent). Item shape required [lastVerifiedResult] with enum success/auth-failed/network-failed/not-tested; optional [lastVerifiedReachable date-time nullable, verifiedBy string nullable]. credentialItem.additionalProperties remains false, credentialItem.required remains [itemId, role]. Filed-and-closed in same PR per PR #102 + PR #106 precedent.

followUps.title: Add vpcPeerings[] top-level array to infrastructure-catalog.schema.json
followUps.openedDuring: EB cross-chat handoff canvas F0B7U37MSDC (2026-06-02 18:29 PDT) — GAP-EXTEND P1 surfaced from d-2026-06-02-prod-spaces-credentials-graph audit
followUps.scope: Additive backwards-compatible extension adding vpcPeerings[] top-level array to infrastructure-catalog.schema.json. Each entry: id (string, e.g. 768a1eb6), envA (env-id ref), envB (env-id ref), status (enum: active, pending-elimination, eliminated), trackedByFollowUp (FU id). Substrate evidence: live peering 768a1eb6 (staging <-> production VPCs) flagged in operator memory for elimination; no canonical way to mark scheduled for elimination, track environment coupling, or detect drift on removal. Proposed shape illustrative; SR refines as canonical authority on infrastructure-catalog evolution.

resolution.summary: Schema extension landed: vpcPeerings[] property added to catalog under schemas/infrastructure-catalog.schema.json at version 1.2.0 (minor-bump-for-additive per PR #102 precedent). Item shape required [id, envA, envB, region, status]; optional [doId, trackedByFollowUp, createdAt, notes]. id pattern ^vpc-peering-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$ matches existing schema convention; envA/envB carry env-id pattern for FK validation. Filed-and-closed in same PR per PR #102 precedent.

followUps.title: Normalize ajv compile/validate invocations across SR workflows and dispatches to include -r for external $ref resolution
followUps.scope: Substrate observation surfaced during PR #106 (Item B vpcPeerings) Step 2: ajv compile failed with 'can't resolve reference https://schemas.luh.tech/_enums/luhtech-enums.schema.json' because the dispatch's compile invocation omitted -r flag. Pre-existing pattern — workflow CI paths use different invocation that resolves refs correctly, but standalone ajv compile/validate from dispatches or local debug fail. Audit all ajv invocations across .github/workflows/*.yml + dispatch templates; normalize to canonical form including all required -r references. Likely affects meta-schema-check.yml, instance-validation.yml, refs-check.yml + any future standalone validation. PR #106 had to work around this mid-dispatch; this FU normalizes the pattern.

resolution.summary: Closed via surgical -r flag addition to roadmap-sync.yml line 50 dependencies.schema.json ajv invocation. Step 1.5 substrate read 2026-06-04 confirmed line 50 was the only confirmed gap of this class within roadmap-sync.yml (lines 35 and 55 have no external $refs and correctly omit -r; line 45 already had correct -r; line 40 venture-summary.schema.json is FILE NOT FOUND — separate FU; line 65 dynamic loop external-ref behavior — separate FU). Two negative-case proofs preceded this fix: PR #106 Item B vpcPeerings v1.2.0 ajv compile failure, PR #119 TRUTH-DELIVERED dispatch authoring miss caught by REFUSE-AND-SURFACE. F0B85LNSTC4 doctrine working: substrate-vs-spec mismatches caught before main pollution. Forward-looking discipline: TRUTH-DELIVERED schema also requires -r forbidden-audit-paint-names flag per session-level work, baked into all subsequent dispatches.

followUps.title: Schema version bump dispatches must include schemas/schema-refs.json rebuild as 4th in-scope file by default
followUps.scope: Substrate observation surfaced during PR #106 (Item B vpcPeerings): refs-check.yml CI failed because schema-refs.json still recorded infrastructure-catalog at v1.1.0 but disk was at v1.2.0. Mid-dispatch scope expansion required to add schemas/schema-refs.json as 4th file and run luhtech-schema refs rebuild. Pattern correction: any future schema version bump dispatch must include schema-refs.json rebuild as scope file from Step 0 (this dispatch Item A applied the lesson). Document the canonical schema-version-bump dispatch template that includes refs rebuild step. PR #102 first established this pattern (terraform-state enum bump); PR #106 missed it; this Item A dispatch applies it correctly; future dispatches should not repeat the omission.

followUps.title: Consider whether resource-access.schema.json should adopt forbidden-audit-paint-names allOf constraint and rename auditTrail
followUps.scope: Substrate observation surfaced during Item A substrate-read: rule.schema.json and truth-delivered.schema.json both enforce allOf forbidden-audit-paint-names constraint, but resource-access.schema.json does not — and declares 'auditTrail' as a legitimate root property. This is per-design for now (audit-paint constraint applies to governance schemas where audit-paint patterns are forbidden, not to access-graph schemas where audit trails are content). Defer decision until governance-vs-content schema boundary policy clarifies portfolio-wide. If resource-access ever adopts the constraint, the auditTrail field requires rename to a structured-changes block (similar to truth-delivered's transitions array). Not blocking any current work; forward-looking only.

followUps.title: Formalize F0B85LNSTC4 canonical-schema authoring doctrine as working-model rule instance per rule.schema.json v1.2.0
followUps.openedDuring: post-Cat-3-arc + eb-infra-hygiene session 2026-06-03 (three iterations of cross-repo execution shape validated)
followUps.scope: F0B85LNSTC4 doctrine (chat-side spec authoring against canonical SR schemas to eliminate reshape friction) is three-times-proven this session: Cat 3 partial reshape arc (PR #105 — 14-error reshape applied chat-side), Cat 3b clean iteration (PR #110 — first-try PASS sandbox validation, doctrine back-propagated), eb-infra-hygiene (PR #114 — third proof, third TRUTH-DELIVERED queued). Substrate-rich for rule pipeline entry. Author as working-model rule at schemas/_instances/rules/RULE-PIPELINE-CANONICAL-SCHEMA-AUTHORING-1.json per rule.schema.json v1.2.0. Tier shape: lifecycleStatus=working-model, activeTier=working-model, occupiableTiers=[working-model, human-loop], terminalTier=human-loop (judgment-class — chat-side authoring discipline cannot be mechanically forced, only enforced via sandbox-validate gate). gateActions.human-loop.gate: chat-side spec authoring MUST sandbox-validate against canonical SR schemas at authoring time, not at execution time. Pairs with skills-meta-layer (PR #113 ca4c856) — both are chat-side authoring discipline.

followUps.title: Design path for SR-chat (browser) to call EB agent tools directly via MCP, or formalize cross-chat substrate-query pattern
followUps.openedDuring: Post-Cat-3b session observation 2026-06-03 (EB agent tools 22 -> 31 across Cat 1 + Cat 2 + Cat 3 partial + Cat 3b; SR-chat browser session has no direct access)
followUps.scope: EB agent tool surface (29 -> 31 across Cat 3 arc this session) lives on EB filesystem as Python modules accessible via eb-agent MCP server. SR-chat (this browser session) cannot call these tools directly — only EB-chat terminal can. Cross-chat coordination via canvases is the proven workaround (validated across 10+ canvases this session for Cat 3 partial + Cat 3b + eb-infra-hygiene arcs). Three design options: (1) publish eb-agent MCP server to public URL (cis-services-1 candidate via deploy-agent HMAC per memory #28); (2) formalize cross-chat substrate-query canvas pattern as canonical when terminal access required; (3) periodic snapshot file uploads (EB-chat runs nightly, posts JSON to Slack canvas; SR-chat reads when needed). Substrate evidence: session 2026-06-03 demonstrated EB agent tools are valuable substrate for SR work — would have caught Cat 3 path-inference miss earlier if SR-chat could query EB filesystem directly via coverage-infrastructure tool. Not blocking current cross-repo execution shape; productivity multiplier when addressed.

followUps.title: Consider canonical mechanism (rule or schema constraint) requiring TRUTH-DELIVERED instance at S8 closure for cross-repo deliverables
followUps.openedDuring: Post-Cat-3b session observation 2026-06-03 (TRUTH-DELIVERED authoring twice-proven: PR #109 + PR #111; third queued for eb-infra-hygiene closure)
followUps.scope: TRUTH-DELIVERED instance authoring at S8 closure is now twice-proven (PR #109 Cat 3 partial closed-with-debt, PR #111 Cat 3b closed-clean) and third instance queued post-eb-infra-hygiene-EB-merge. Pattern works end-to-end but no canonical mechanism enforces it — relies on chat-side memory of the pattern. Two options: (1) author as governance rule (working-model -> human-loop) requiring chat-side checklist at S8 transition; (2) schema constraint on deliverable.schema.json requiring TRUTH-DELIVERED instance presence before transition to S8-closed validates. Option 2 is stronger (structurally unrepresentable if missing per memory #15 documented-override-not-compliance pattern). Defer until pattern proven on 5+ deliverables across multiple session-types to validate the constraint shape. Forward-looking, not blocking.

followUps.title: Investigate venture-summary.schema.json invocation in roadmap-sync.yml — FILE NOT FOUND on disk during 2026-06-04 substrate read
followUps.scope: During Step 1.5 substrate read of roadmap-sync.yml, line 40 invocation targets schemas/venture-summary.schema.json but the file is not present at that path on disk. Two possibilities: (1) workflow invocation is dead code — file was deleted or renamed without corresponding workflow cleanup; (2) workflow invocation has wrong path — file exists under a different name or location. As written, the line 40 invocation would fail at runtime if the workflow ran against any venture-summary instance. Investigation work: search repo for venture-summary*.schema.json files (any version), trace any references to determine intent, then either DELETE the line 40 invocation (if dead code) or FIX the schema path (if relocated). Closes after workflow line 40 reflects substrate truth.

followUps.title: Audit .github/workflows/instance-validation.yml ajv invocations for same -r flag gap class
followUps.scope: Step 1.5 substrate read flagged that instance-validation.yml lines 35 + 57 use npx -y ajv-cli@5 validate without visible -r flags. The schemas being validated (deliverable.schema.json + rule.schema.json) have known external refs to forbidden-audit-paint-names.schema.json per session-level work. Audit: read instance-validation.yml verbatim, walk external $refs for each referenced schema, identify any invocation that needs -r and currently lacks it. Same fix class as the line 50 dependencies.schema.json gap closed by d-2026-06-04-sr-ajv-r-flag-workflow-normalization, but applied to a different workflow file — kept out-of-scope from that deliverable per memory #18 branch scope-lock and filed here for separate execution.

followUps.title: Analyze roadmap-sync.yml line 65 dynamic-schema-name loop for external-ref behavior
followUps.scope: Step 1.5 substrate read flagged line 65 of roadmap-sync.yml uses a dynamic $schema_name variable inside an examples loop — external-ref behavior could not be statically determined during the read because the schema is selected at runtime per iteration. Analysis work: enumerate all schema files the loop targets at runtime, build the same external-refs table as Step 1.5 substrate read produced for the static lines 35-55, then determine whether the loop's ajv invocation needs -r handling (and if so, what shape — likely a per-iteration -r flag set built dynamically). Forward-looking, not blocking — line 65 may already be functionally correct depending on which schemas the loop iterates over.

followUps.title: Doctrine refinement: EB-pre-SR-ship pattern observed twice — codify acceptance criteria or enforce SR-first
followUps.scope: Cross-repo doctrine specifies SR-anchor-first, then EB executes, then SR closes with TRUTH-DELIVERED. Pattern of EB shipping to main BEFORE SR anchor landing has now been observed twice this week: PR #290 (coverage-nested-resources, 2026-06-03 — captured in TRUTH-DELIVERED #3 overrideRecord) and PR #294 (catalog-ground-truth-atomic, 2026-06-04 — captured in TRUTH-DELIVERED #6 overrideRecord, this PR). Two instances is substrate-rich enough to trigger doctrine refinement decision. Two paths flat: (1) codify acceptance criteria for EB-pre-SR-ship as a valid deviation — define which deliverable classes allow it, what compensating control is required (e.g. retroactive SR anchor landing within N hours), how TRUTH-DELIVERED captures the deviation. OR (2) tighten doctrine to enforce SR-first — design EB CI check that requires target deliverable have SR instance before allowing merge. Path (2) requires SR-EB cross-repo signal (EB CI checking SR schemas/_instances/deliverables/). Decision is L4-class. This FU surfaces the choice; does not pre-decide.

followUps.title: Capture cross-schema FK-edge current truth as a schema-bound graph manifest (infra schema set)
followUps.scope: The infra schema set (infrastructure-catalog, resource-access, environment-map, workflow-registry, tech-stack) already carries FK relationships, but they live only as prose description strings (e.g. resource-access.credentialItem.secretRef "FK -> infrastructure-catalog secret.name"; environmentPosture.environmentRef "references catalog /definitions/environment id"; accessGrant.resourceRef into catalog server/environment/database/bucket; environment-map.catalogRef; resource-access.catalogRef). They are not machine-declared, not validated, not queryable as a graph. Capture the CURRENT edge truth as a schema-bound relationship/graph manifest (new schema + instance) so the edges are canonical truth rather than rediscovered live each session. Do-first / concrete: buildable from substrate readable today, no external research gate. Observed cost: relationship structure re-litigated repeatedly in chat. Note: workflow-registry is currently FK-orphaned from the infra set — capture should record that gap, not invent an edge. Build is a separate L4-gated deliverable; this FU is the capture record.

followUps.title: Establish canonical enum-registry schema sibling to acronyms-catalog; replace in-place enum redeclaration with $ref
followUps.scope: Enumerated vocabularies are defined in-place, per schema, in a vacuum: workflow-registry lane/gate-forms/category; resource-access accessMethod.category + accessMethod.mechanism + hostPosture.posture + envRoute.chain.stage; environment-map sshPosture/complianceStatus/type. Each redeclared where used; no shared canonical source; drift between schemas undetectable. Establish a canonical enum-registry schema as a sibling to acronyms-catalog.json (mirror that pattern), and have schemas $ref the registry instead of inlining enums. Greenfield-foundation / scaling dividend: one edit propagates portfolio-wide; new schemas inherit canonical vocabularies. Pre-build reads required (deferred to build): live acronyms-catalog.schema.json (mirror pattern) + Alkio Graph surface (connected graph-vector state). Build is a separate L4-gated deliverable; this FU is the capture record. Overlaps FU-FEATURE-DEC-INFRA-GRAPH-ARCHITECTURE-1 (P2, SR) at the graph layer — reconcile amend-vs-extend at build.

followUps.title: Decide intended relationship between ectropy-ai/schemas scoped emit and luh-tech canonical (workflow-registry + 5 sibling schemas)
followUps.scope: SR-CLI surfaced during d-2026-06-19-sr-workflow-registry-formalization (SR PR #182) that ectropy-ai/schemas carries its own workflow-registry.schema.json under an intentional scoped $id namespace: https://schemas.luh.tech/ectropy/workflow-registry.schema.json (verified 6/6 schemas in that repo scoped, 0 canonical; README confirms Ectropy-scoped intent, consumed by @ectropy/core + Ectropy-Business). The ectropy emit is at v1.0.0 (category free-string, no lane/gate/chain/canonicalDeploy) while the luh-tech canonical is now v1.2.0. This corrects SR-chat earlier framing that treated the emit as a third leg of main-repo-emit version parity — the emit is a SEPARATE scoped schema, not a mirror, so version parity was the wrong target. Decision required (L4-class, cross-repo with ectropy-ai/schemas): (A) ectropy emit tracks canonical field-set -> bring to 1.2.0 parity, own PR on ectropy-ai/schemas, package 0.4.0->0.4.1; (C) intentional independent scoped namespace, no parity obligation; plus the latent question of whether ectropy schemas should $ref the canonical rather than copy. Reject (B) version-only bump — would make emit claim 1.2.0 while carrying 1.0.0 fields (a lying version). Overlaps FU-SR-CANONICAL-ENUM-REGISTRY-1 (cross-repo enum/vocab in a vacuum) and FU-SR-INFRA-SCHEMA-GRAPH-CURRENT-TRUTH-1 (FK-edge truth). Capture-only; resolution is separately L4-gated.
